description: Abdominal pain in a pregnant woman; defensible ESI 2 (any pregnant abdominal pain) or ESI 3 (stable presentation, single resource workup).

patient.name: Rachel K., 28F
patient.chief_complaint: Right lower quadrant abdominal pain for 4 hours, intermittent, no fever, no vaginal bleeding. Currently 22 weeks pregnant, prior uncomplicated pregnancy. Tender RLQ but no rigidity, no rebound.
patient.history: G2P1, 22 weeks gestation, prior C-section, otherwise healthy

rubric_notes: Pregnancy = higher acuity floor for any abdominal complaint. Differential includes appendicitis (shifted right and superior in pregnancy), placental abruption (uncommon at 22w but possible), ovarian torsion, preterm labor. Defensible ESI 2 (any pregnant abdominal pain warrants prompt evaluation including OB consult) or ESI 3 (stable vitals, no peritonitis, single resource workup with ultrasound). Most EDs lean ESI 2 for pregnancy.
